story: In the interview room, the wolf admits he committed the crimes. Mr. R, a slick lawyer, says innocence does not matter; he can recast the wolf as a victim of anti-wolf prejudice, win a lighter sentence, and take half the profits from interviews, TV, and a book. Hearing this, the wolf would rather face punishment than let Mr. R turn him into a fake tragic hero.
implausible_knowledge: Hearing this, the wolf would rather face punishment than let Mr. R turn him into a fake tragic hero.
question: What will Mr. R most likely do next to try to secure the arrangement?
answer_with_knowledge: Stop pressing the deal and back away instead of presenting the papers.
answer_without_knowledge: Open his briefcase, slide over the contract, and push the wolf to sign.
